title: Test T-3: Install Vitest + unit-test rebuildOrder (delete-after-reorder repro)
description:
## Why
Phase-CP1 fix added `rebuildOrder(oldOrder, oldRules, newRules)` in `frontend/src/lib/utils.js` to fix the `script.order` desync on delete-after-reorder. **There is no frontend test runner.** Any future regression goes undetected.

## What
1. Install Vitest (and `@testing-library/svelte` if needed for component-level tests later).
2. Wire `npm test` script.
3. Add unit tests for `rebuildOrder` covering:
   - reorder then delete (the original repro)
   - delete then reorder
   - add new rule (appends to order)
   - combined add+delete+reorder in one tick
   - `RawBlock` order entries preserved verbatim

## Acceptance
- `npm test` runs Vitest and reports green.
- All 5+ cases passing.
- Reverting `rebuildOrder` to the old extract-permute-renumber logic makes the suite go red.
- Effort: S.

## References
- `.full-review/03a-testing-findings.md` (T-3)
- `.full-review/CRITICAL-FIXES-APPLIED.md` Fix 4

title: Test T-1: pytest regression for outbound IMAP TLS verification
description:
## Why
Phase-CP1 fix in `backend/imap_client.py` installed `ssl.create_default_context()` for outbound IMAP. **No test guards it.** A future refactor reverting to `ssl._create_stdlib_context()` re-opens MITM credential theft (CWE-295, CVSS 9.1) with no test failure.

## What
Add `backend/tests/test_imap_tls.py` (or similar) asserting:
1. `IMAP4_SSL` is invoked with an `ssl_context` whose `verify_mode == ssl.CERT_REQUIRED`.
2. `check_hostname is True`.
3. With `AYS_IMAP_INSECURE=1`, the unverified context is used **and** a warning is logged.

Mock `imaplib.IMAP4_SSL` to capture the kwargs.

## Acceptance
- 3 pytest cases passing.
- Removing the `create_default_context()` line in `imap_client.py` makes the suite go red.
- Effort: S.

## References
- `.full-review/03a-testing-findings.md` (T-1)
- `.full-review/CRITICAL-FIXES-APPLIED.md` Fix 1

title: Drop `async` from POST /api/scripts/import (event-loop blocker)
description:
## Why
`import_script()` in `backend/app.py` is declared `async def` but calls **synchronous** `SieveClient` methods. A single slow upload blocks the entire uvicorn event loop — every other request stalls until it finishes.

## What
Drop the `async` keyword from the route handler. FastAPI runs sync handlers in a threadpool automatically, isolating slow I/O from the loop.

## Acceptance
- `POST /api/scripts/import` is a plain `def` (not `async def`).
- A slow upload no longer blocks concurrent calls to `/api/auth/status` (manual smoke test ok, automated test tracked in `crit-5`).
- Effort: XS (one-line change).

## References
- `.full-review/02b-performance-findings.md` (Perf C1)
- `.full-review/04a-framework-findings.md` (Fwk C-1)
- `.full-review/05-final-report.md` Sprint 1 item 1

title: Test T-4: Integration test for event-loop blocking on /api/scripts/import
description:
## Why
Once `crit-1` drops `async` from `import_script`, nothing prevents a well-meaning refactor from reintroducing `async def` later. Without an integration test, the regression re-blocks the event loop silently and only surfaces under production load.

## What
Using `httpx.AsyncClient` against the live ASGI app (or `TestClient` with a parallel runner):
1. Fire N concurrent slow `POST /api/scripts/import` calls (mock SieveClient to sleep e.g. 2s).
2. Assert another endpoint (e.g. `GET /api/auth/status`) responds in well under `N * 2s` — proves they aren't serialized on the loop.
3. Threshold: total wall-clock under `(N / threadpool_size + 1) * 2s` with margin.

## Acceptance
- Test fails if route is reverted to `async def`.
- Test passes today with the fix applied.
- Effort: M.

## Depends on
`crit-1` (the fix has to ship before this test can pass).

## References
- `.full-review/03a-testing-findings.md` (T-4)

title: Test T-2: pytest regression for IMAP folder-name CRLF injection guard
description:
## Why
Phase-CP1 fix in `backend/imap_client.py::create_folder` rejects names matching `[\r\n\x00"\\]` before issuing the IMAP `CREATE`. **No test guards it.** Any future refactor that drops the validator silently re-opens CRLF/command injection (Sec H-3).

## What
Add pytest cases asserting `create_folder()` raises `ValueError` (or equivalent) for each payload:
- bare `\r`, `\n`, `\r\n`
- NUL byte
- embedded `"` and `\`
- the PoC payloads documented in `.full-review/02a-security-findings.md` Sec H-3

And asserts a known-good folder name passes.

## Acceptance
- All injection payloads rejected; benign names accepted.
- Removing the validator makes the suite go red.
- Effort: S.

## References
- `.full-review/03a-testing-findings.md` (T-2)
- `.full-review/CRITICAL-FIXES-APPLIED.md` Fix 6

title: Test T-11: pytest for script_to_json / json_to_script round-trip
description:
## Why
The Pydantic ↔ dataclass ↔ Sieve conversion paths (`script_to_json`, `json_to_script` in `sieve_transform.py`) are the boundary where frontend JSON becomes a Sieve AST and vice versa. **They are untested.** Bugs here silently corrupt user rules during save/load.

## What
Extend `backend/tests/test_sieve_transform.py` with parametrized round-trip cases for every fixture in `backend/test_scripts/*.sieve`:
```
parse(text) → script_to_json → json_to_script → generate == text
```
Also add targeted tests for:
- empty rule list
- rule with only `RawBlock` entries
- mixed Rule + RawBlock ordering preservation

## Acceptance
- Round-trip stable across all fixtures.
- A deliberate breakage of `script_to_json` is caught.
- Effort: S.

## References
- `.full-review/03a-testing-findings.md` (T-11)

title: Add response_model= everywhere + typed SaveScriptRequest DTO
description:
## Why
No route declares `response_model=`, so OpenAPI shows empty/wrong schemas and Pydantic doesn't strip extra fields from responses — accidental field leaks possible (e.g., password hash, internal state). Worse: `SaveScriptRequest.rules: list[Any]` defeats validation entirely on the most security-sensitive endpoint.

## What
1. Define typed Pydantic DTOs for request and response on every route. Notable:
   - `RuleDTO` and `RawBlockDTO` (union as `RuleOrRawDTO`).
   - `SaveScriptRequest.rules: list[RuleOrRawDTO]`.
   - `ScriptResponse` for read paths.
2. Add `response_model=...` to every `@app.get/post/put/delete`.
3. Use `response_model_exclude_none=True` where appropriate.

## Acceptance
- `/openapi.json` shows real schemas for every route.
- Sending an extra unknown rule field is rejected (or stripped) deterministically.
- Effort: M.

## References
- `.full-review/04a-framework-findings.md` (Fwk H-2, Fwk H-4)

title: Fix SSRF / DNS-rebinding in _validate_host (resolve-then-connect-by-IP)
description:
## Why
`_validate_host()` has a TOCTOU window: it resolves DNS, checks the IP isn't private/loopback/link-local, then the IMAP/Sieve client re-resolves DNS and opens the connection. An attacker can flip the DNS answer between the check and the connect (DNS rebinding) and reach internal hosts.

## What
Resolve the hostname **once**, then connect by the resolved IP:
1. `getaddrinfo(host)` → pick first acceptable IP.
2. Validate IP against the private/loopback/link-local/multicast/IPv4-mapped IPv6 deny-list.
3. Open the socket to the IP literal.
4. Keep the original hostname only for TLS SNI and certificate hostname verification (`server_hostname=host`).

Apply to both IMAP and ManageSieve client paths.

## Acceptance
- A DNS server that flips between public and private answers no longer reaches private destinations.
- Cert verification still works (hostname checked against SNI, not IP).
- Effort: M.

## References
- `.full-review/02a-security-findings.md` (Sec H-1)

title: Request-body size limit middleware + Field(max_length=) on DTOs
description:
## Why
JSON endpoints (especially `/api/scripts/save` and `/api/scripts/import`) have **no body-size cap**. An attacker can POST a multi-GB body, exhausting memory before Pydantic even sees it.

## What
1. Add a Starlette middleware (or ASGI wrapper) that rejects requests where `Content-Length` exceeds `AYS_MAX_BODY_BYTES` (default 1 MiB) with HTTP 413. Also stream-check the actual body to defend against missing/lying headers.
2. Add `Field(max_length=N)` to every `str`/`list` field in request DTOs (`SaveScriptRequest`, `LoginRequest`, etc.) for defense in depth.

## Acceptance
- 2 MiB POST to `/api/scripts/save` returns 413, not OOM.
- Existing legitimate scripts (largest fixture in `test_scripts/`) still save.
- Effort: S.

## References
- `.full-review/02a-security-findings.md` (Sec H-4)
- `.full-review/04a-framework-findings.md` (Fwk H-3)

title: Dockerfile hardening: USER 1000, HEALTHCHECK, tini, digest pin
description:
## Why
The current Dockerfile:
- Runs as **root** (container escape blast radius).
- Has **no HEALTHCHECK** (orchestrators can't tell if the app is alive).
- Has **no PID-1 reaper** (zombie processes on signal/restart).
- Uses **floating image tags** (supply-chain risk: today's `python:3.12-slim` ≠ tomorrow's).

## What
1. Create non-root user (`useradd -u 1000 -r ays`) and `chown` the app dir; `USER 1000:1000`.
2. Install `tini` and set `ENTRYPOINT ["/usr/bin/tini","--"]`.
3. Add `HEALTHCHECK CMD curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:8091/healthz || exit 1` (consumer of `med-4`).
4. Pin base images by digest: `FROM python:3.12-slim@sha256:...` and same for the Node builder.
5. Add Renovate/Dependabot config (or note in `trk-cicd`) so digest pins get refreshed.

## Acceptance
- `docker inspect` shows non-root user, healthcheck, tini entrypoint, digest-pinned base.
- Image still builds and starts cleanly.
- Effort: S.

## References
- `.full-review/04b-cicd-findings.md` (CICD-4)

title: Trusted-proxy rate-limit IP detection
description:
## Why
The rate limiter reads `X-Real-IP` (or `X-Forwarded-For`) directly. **Any client on a direct connection can spoof this header**, trivially bypassing the 5-attempts-per-5-min login throttle by rotating fake IPs.

## What
Add `AYS_TRUSTED_PROXIES` env var (CSV of CIDRs, e.g. `127.0.0.1/32,10.0.0.0/8`). The rate limiter computes the effective client IP as:
1. If `request.client.host` is in a trusted CIDR → honor the rightmost trusted entry in `X-Forwarded-For` (or `X-Real-IP`).
2. Else → use `request.client.host` directly, ignore proxy headers.

Document behavior in README env-var table.

## Acceptance
- Direct connection: spoofed `X-Real-IP` is ignored.
- Behind a reverse proxy in the trusted list: real client IP is honored, throttle works per-user.
- Effort: S.

## References
- `.full-review/02a-security-findings.md` (Sec H-7)
- `.full-review/04a-framework-findings.md` (Fwk H-6)

title: Backend toolchain: ruff + basedpyright + pre-commit
description:
## Why
Backend has zero linter, formatter, or type checker. Today's bugs (e.g., the unverified TLS, the ReDoS) would have been caught by any of these earlier.

## What
1. Add `pyproject.toml` with:
   - `[tool.ruff]` — format + lint config; opinionated default rule set.
   - `[tool.basedpyright]` — strict mode with sensible escape hatches.
2. Add `.pre-commit-config.yaml` running ruff format, ruff check, basedpyright, and a `pytest -q` fast check on the backend module.
3. Wire `pre-commit install` into CONTRIBUTING.md.
4. Add a lint step to CI (`high-6`) so PRs fail on lint/type errors.

Paste-ready config is in `.full-review/04a-framework-findings.md` (Fwk toolchain bundle).

## Acceptance
- `ruff format --check` and `ruff check` pass on the current backend.
- `basedpyright` runs (existing errors filed individually or scoped down initially).
- CI fails on intentional formatting break.
- Effort: S.

## References
- `.full-review/04a-framework-findings.md` Fwk toolchain bundle

title: GitHub Actions CI workflow (pytest + frontend build)
description:
## Why
No CI gate exists. The new pytest suite is invisible to PRs; the frontend `npm run build` can silently break on merge. The whole point of having tests is that they fail loudly when broken.

## What
Write `.github/workflows/ci.yml` that on push/PR:
1. Sets up Python 3.12 + Node 20.
2. Installs `backend/requirements.txt` and `backend/requirements-dev.txt`.
3. Runs `python -m pytest backend/tests/ -v --tb=short`.
4. `cd frontend && npm ci && npm run build`.
5. (After `crit-4`) `cd frontend && npm test`.
6. Blocks merge on red status.

A paste-ready workflow is in `.full-review/04b-cicd-findings.md`.

## Acceptance
- CI runs on every PR.
- A deliberately broken test fails the workflow.
- Effort: S.

## Depends on
`high-5` (needs `requirements-dev.txt` to exist).

## References
- `.full-review/04b-cicd-findings.md` (CICD-3)

title: Add CSRF middleware (starlette-csrf or equivalent)
description:
## Why
Cookie-based auth + no CSRF token means any malicious site can issue authenticated POST/PUT/DELETE to `/api/*` in a logged-in user's browser. SameSite=Strict on the session cookie helps but isn't a substitute (third-party iframes, link prefetch, browser quirks).

## What
1. Install `starlette-csrf` (or roll a double-submit-cookie pattern).
2. Issue a CSRF token alongside the session cookie on login.
3. Require `X-CSRF-Token` header on all mutating routes (`POST`, `PUT`, `DELETE`).
4. Exempt `POST /api/auth/login` (pre-auth, no session yet).
5. Update the SPA `api.js` fetcher to read the token from a cookie/meta tag and inject the header.
6. Add the header to `high-2` CORS `allow_headers` list (already included in that draft).

## Acceptance
- A mutating request missing the header returns 403.
- The SPA continues to work after `api.js` is updated.
- Effort: M.

## References
- `.full-review/02a-security-findings.md` (Sec H-6)
- `.full-review/04a-framework-findings.md` (Fwk H-7)

title: Add socket timeout to managesieve_client.py (close threadpool DoS)
description:
## Why
The ManageSieve client (port 4190) has **no socket timeout**. A slow/blackhole mail server hangs the connection indefinitely. Phase 2 measured 100% unavailability at ~8 concurrent slow requests (threadpool exhaustion). IMAP was fixed at Phase-CP1; ManageSieve is the matching unfixed half.

## What
In `backend/managesieve_client.py`:
- 10s connect timeout (`socket.create_connection(..., timeout=10)`).
- 30s read/write timeout (`sock.settimeout(30)` after handshake).
- Optional: env-var overrides (`AYS_SIEVE_CONNECT_TIMEOUT`, `AYS_SIEVE_IO_TIMEOUT`) for slow self-hosted setups.

## Acceptance
- A blackhole sieve endpoint causes a clean `socket.timeout` after 10s, not an indefinite hang.
- Existing happy-path flows unaffected.
- Effort: XS.

## References
- `.full-review/02a-security-findings.md` (Sec H-2)
- `.full-review/02b-performance-findings.md` (Perf H1)

title: Document AYS_IMAP_INSECURE env var (README, docker-compose, AGENTS.md)
description:
## Why
`AYS_IMAP_INSECURE` was added at Phase-CP1 as the TLS-verification opt-out for self-signed test setups. It's **undocumented** in every user-facing surface. A new self-hoster with a self-signed cert will not discover the escape hatch and will assume the app is broken.

## What
1. Add row to README env-var table (with a `⚠️ Insecure — only for testing` note).
2. Mention it in `docker-compose.yml` as a commented-out env entry with the warning.
3. Reference in `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` security section.
4. Mention in `backend/AGENTS.md`.

## Acceptance
- Grep for `AYS_IMAP_INSECURE` returns hits in README, docker-compose, ARCHITECTURE, and backend/AGENTS.
- Effort: XS.

## References
- `.full-review/03b-documentation-findings.md` (DOC-4)

title: Tighten CORS: explicit allow_methods + allow_headers
description:
## Why
Current `CORSMiddleware` config is `allow_methods=['*']` + `allow_headers=['*']` + `allow_credentials=True`. With credentialed CORS, wildcards expand the cross-origin attack surface unnecessarily.

## What
Replace wildcards with explicit allow-lists:
- `allow_methods=['GET','POST','PUT','DELETE','OPTIONS']`
- `allow_headers=['Content-Type','Cookie','X-CSRF-Token']` (last entry anticipates `high-8`).

Keep `allow_credentials=True` and `allow_origins=AYS_CORS_ORIGINS.split(',')` as-is.

## Acceptance
- Browser preflight still succeeds for the SPA.
- Unlisted methods/headers rejected.
- Effort: XS.

## References
- `.full-review/02a-security-findings.md` (Sec H-5)
- `.full-review/04a-framework-findings.md` (Fwk H-5)

title: Add requirements-dev.txt + CONTRIBUTING.md test section + .editorconfig
description:
## Why
The new pytest suite is invisible without documented dev deps. New contributors have no entry point.

## What
1. Create `backend/requirements-dev.txt` listing `pytest>=8` (and `pytest-asyncio` once `crit-5` lands, `vitest` is npm so n/a here).
2. Add a **Running tests** section to `CONTRIBUTING.md`:
   ```
   cd backend
   pip install -r requirements.txt -r requirements-dev.txt
   python -m pytest tests/ -v
   ```
3. Add `.editorconfig` at repo root matching existing style: 4-space Python (`*.py`), 2-space JS/Svelte/JSON/YAML (`*.{js,svelte,json,yml,yaml}`), LF line endings, trim trailing whitespace.

## Acceptance
- A fresh clone can run the suite from CONTRIBUTING instructions alone.
- Editors honor `.editorconfig` for indentation.
- Effort: S.

## References
- `.full-review/03b-documentation-findings.md` (Doc-2, Doc-3)
- `.full-review/04a-framework-findings.md` (Fwk M-15)

title: Disable /docs /redoc /openapi.json in production
description:
## Why
FastAPI's auto-docs are public by default. `/docs` leaks the full API surface (routes, params, schemas) to unauthenticated visitors and gives attackers a free recon map.

## What
Gate auto-docs on an env var:
```python
is_dev = os.getenv('AYS_ENV', 'prod') == 'dev'
app = FastAPI(
    docs_url='/docs' if is_dev else None,
    redoc_url='/redoc' if is_dev else None,
    openapi_url='/openapi.json' if is_dev else None,
)
```
Document `AYS_ENV` in README env-var table.

## Acceptance
- Default deploy: `/docs` returns 404.
- `AYS_ENV=dev`: `/docs` works.
- Effort: XS.

## References
- `.full-review/02a-security-findings.md` (Sec M-5)
- `.full-review/04a-framework-findings.md` (Fwk M-4)
- `.full-review/04b-cicd-findings.md` (CICD-6)

title: [Tracker] Framework Medium/Low backlog (~24 findings)
description:
## Purpose
Umbrella tracker for remaining Framework/language Medium/Low findings.

## Source
[`./full-review/04a-framework-findings.md`](.full-review/04a-framework-findings.md).

## Already filed elsewhere
- Fwk C-1 → `crit-1`.
- H-2, H-4 → `high-12`.
- H-3 → `high-3`. H-5 → `high-2`. H-6 → `high-9`. H-7 → `high-8`.
- M-2 → `med-1`. M-3 → `med-2`. M-4 → `high-11`. M-5 → `med-3`. M-15 → `high-5`.
- Toolchain bundle → `high-13`.

## What's in here
~20+ Medium + ~5 Low Framework findings. Notable open:
- **Fwk H-1** `auth_status` is `async def` for no reason (same anti-pattern as `crit-1`; promote to individual if it becomes load-bearing).
- **Fwk M-9** `json_to_script` defensive parsing tightening.
- **Fwk M-17** Frontend `Math.random()` IDs — replace with `crypto.randomUUID()`.
- **Fwk M-18** Vite production build unconfigured (sourcemaps, minification, target).
- **Fwk M-1** `auth.py` typing tightening, dataclass slots, etc.

title: Add SECURITY.md and CHANGELOG.md
description:
## Why
- `SECURITY.md` is missing — GitHub auto-promotes the *Report a vulnerability* UI when present, and currently there's no documented disclosure channel.
- `CHANGELOG.md` is missing — self-hosters can't tell what changed between versions or whether they need to act on a release.

## What
1. `SECURITY.md` covering: supported versions, how to report (email + GitHub Security advisory), expected response time, scope.
2. `CHANGELOG.md` in Keep-a-Changelog format. Backfill with the recent fix history (post-Phase-CP1 entries are easy: TLS, ReDoS, CRLF, `python-multipart` CVE, parser fixes, frontend order fix).

## Acceptance
- Both files exist at repo root.
- GitHub's Security tab shows the policy.
- Effort: XS.

## References
- `.full-review/03b-documentation-findings.md` (DOC-8, DOC-18)

title: [Tracker] Architecture Medium/Low backlog (~14 findings)
description:
## Purpose
Umbrella tracker for remaining Medium/Low Architecture findings not in Sprint 1–3.

## Source
[`./full-review/01b-architecture-findings.md`](.full-review/01b-architecture-findings.md).

## Already filed elsewhere
- Arch M2 (app.py god-module) — covered by `med-1`.

## What's in here
~5 Medium + ~9 Low Architecture findings. Themes: dependency direction nits, missing abstractions, opportunities for clearer module boundaries.

Also preserves the **Architectural Strengths** list from the review — do NOT change those without consideration. See section in `.full-review/05-final-report.md`.

title: [Tracker] CI/CD Medium/Low backlog (~15 findings)
description:
## Purpose
Umbrella tracker for remaining Medium/Low CI/CD findings.

## Source
[`./full-review/04b-cicd-findings.md`](.full-review/04b-cicd-findings.md).

## Already filed elsewhere
- CICD-2 → `med-7`. CICD-3 → `high-6`. CICD-4 → `high-10`. CICD-5 → keep here (Trivy/Grype scan). CICD-6 → `high-11`. CICD-7, CICD-8 → `med-4`. CICD-14 → `med-7`.

## What's in here
~15 Medium + Low CI/CD findings. Notable open:
- **CICD-5** Container image scanning (Trivy/Grype) in CI.
- **CICD-13** docker-compose env hardening (read-only root FS, drop capabilities, no-new-privileges).
- **CICD-17** docker-compose `restart: unless-stopped` and named volumes.
- Workflow caching for pip/npm to speed CI.
- Dependabot/Renovate config for Python + npm + Docker base images.

title: Replace hand-rolled static-file route with FastAPI StaticFiles
description:
## Why
`serve_frontend()` is a hand-rolled SPA fallback with a manual path-traversal guard. `StaticFiles(html=True)` does the same with battle-tested upstream code. Less custom security-sensitive code = fewer bugs.

## What
Mount `StaticFiles(directory=static_dir, html=True)` at `/` and remove the custom route. Verify the SPA fallback (unknown deep links → index.html) still works as expected — that's the `html=True` behavior.

Do this **after** `med-1` so it's a clean edit in `routers/static.py`.

## Acceptance
- All paths the old route served still serve (especially deep links and asset paths).
- Path-traversal payloads still 404 (Starlette enforces this).
- Effort: S.

## Depends on
`med-1`.

## References
- `.full-review/04a-framework-findings.md` (Fwk M-3)

title: [Tracker] Testing Medium/Low backlog (T-5..T-18 not yet filed)
description:
## Purpose
Umbrella tracker for Testing findings T-5 through T-18 not yet filed individually.

## Source
[`./full-review/03a-testing-findings.md`](.full-review/03a-testing-findings.md).

## Already filed elsewhere
- T-1 → `crit-2`, T-2 → `crit-3`, T-3 → `crit-4`, T-4 → `crit-5`.
- T-11 → `high-4`.

## What's in here
T-5..T-10 (coverage gaps for the unfixed Sec High items — will become individual issues as the Sec issues land), T-12..T-18 (frontend test runner setup, e2e tests, regression fixtures, fuzzing on the parser, etc).

## Suggested workflow
When `high-1`..`high-9` close, file matching test-lock issues here as new criticals (mirror the pattern used for `crit-2..crit-5`).

title: [Tracker] Documentation Medium/Low backlog (~15 findings)
description:
## Purpose
Umbrella tracker for remaining Medium/Low Documentation findings.

## Source
[`./full-review/03b-documentation-findings.md`](.full-review/03b-documentation-findings.md).

## Already filed elsewhere
- DOC-2, DOC-3 → `high-5`.
- DOC-4 → `high-14`.
- DOC-6 → `med-8`.
- DOC-8, DOC-18 → `med-6`.

## What's in here
~12 Medium + ~3 Low Doc findings. Notable:
- **DOC-1** README missing plaintext/single-tenant caveat in Security section.
- **DOC-5** FastAPI auto-docs prod-exposure undocumented (resolved by `high-11` shipping; this doc fix may be moot).
- **DOC-7** `backend/fetch_grak_script.py` committed PII without doc — investigate and either delete, anonymize, or document.
- **DOC-9..DOC-20** Misc README/AGENTS accuracy fixes.

title: Fix docs/ARCHITECTURE.md: dead file references + non-existent endpoint
description:
## Why
`docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` references files that don't exist (likely renamed/deleted during refactors) and at least one non-existent API endpoint. Doc credibility loss + misleads contributors.

## What
1. Audit every file path mentioned in `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` against the actual tree; fix or remove dead references.
2. Audit every API endpoint mentioned against `backend/app.py` (post-`med-1`: `backend/routers/*.py`); fix or remove.
3. Verify diagrams (if any) match the real request flow.

## Acceptance
- A reader following the doc finds every referenced file/endpoint.
- Effort: S.

## References
- `.full-review/03b-documentation-findings.md` (DOC-6)

title: Code-split SPA routes via Vite manualChunks + dynamic imports
description:
## Why
Single ~200 KB bundle includes every route on first paint. The Rule Editor is rarely the landing page yet ships on every visit. Expected 30–50% first-paint improvement.

## What
1. Configure `vite.config.js` `build.rollupOptions.output.manualChunks` to split vendor (Svelte runtime), route shells, and Rule Editor heavy bits.
2. Wrap each route component in dynamic import via Svelte `{#await import('./routes/X.svelte')}` blocks (or the project's router pattern).
3. Verify the lazy chunks load on first navigation, not on first paint.

## Acceptance
- Initial bundle (transferred) drops by ~30%+ measured by `npm run build` output sizes.
- All routes still navigate without errors.
- Effort: M.

## References
- `.full-review/02b-performance-findings.md` (Perf H4)

title: Add /healthz endpoint + structured audit logging
description:
## Why
No operational observability. Orchestrators can't liveness-probe the app (Dockerfile HEALTHCHECK from `high-10` needs a target). Security-relevant events (login, logout, script save/delete) aren't logged in any structured way.

## What
1. **GET /healthz** → `{"ok": true}` (200). Does NOT touch IMAP/Sieve — pure liveness.
2. (Optional) **GET /readyz** → checks reachability to configured mail server, 200/503.
3. Configure `logging` with a JSON formatter (e.g., `python-json-logger`).
4. Emit `audit` records for: login success, login failure (with IP), logout, script save, script delete, folder create.
5. Document `/healthz` for reverse-proxy upstream checks.

## Acceptance
- `/healthz` returns 200 with the app alive.
- Login attempts produce a parseable JSON log line.
- Effort: S.

## References
- `.full-review/04b-cicd-findings.md` (CICD-7, CICD-8)

title: [Tracker] Performance Medium/Low backlog (~11 findings)
description:
## Purpose
Umbrella tracker for remaining Medium/Low Performance findings.

## Source
[`./full-review/02b-performance-findings.md`](.full-review/02b-performance-findings.md).

## Already filed elsewhere
- Perf C1 → `crit-1`.
- Perf H1 → `high-1`.
- Perf H4 → `med-5`.

## What's in here
~7 Medium + ~5 Low Perf findings. Notable open: **Perf H2** (per-request mail-server connection overhead 150–400 ms) and **Perf H3** (threadpool exhaustion at ~8 concurrent slow requests). H2 is worth promoting to an individual issue once the architecture decision (pool vs. keep-alive vs. accept) is made.

File individual issues for items you decide to act on; close the tracker when reduced to deferred Lows.

title: Multi-arch GHCR publish workflow (amd64 + arm64)
description:
## Why
No published image. Self-hosters must `git clone && docker compose build`. That excludes anyone who wants a one-line `docker run` or who's on a Raspberry Pi / Apple Silicon and would benefit from a pre-built arm64 image.

## What
Add `.github/workflows/release.yml` triggered on `v*` tag push:
1. `docker/setup-qemu-action` + `docker/setup-buildx-action`.
2. `docker/login-action` against `ghcr.io` with `GITHUB_TOKEN`.
3. `docker/build-push-action` with `platforms: linux/amd64,linux/arm64`, tags `ghcr.io/derekslenk/areyousievious:{tag},latest`.
4. (Optional) `cosign` sign the image with keyless OIDC.
5. Update README quick-start with the `docker run ghcr.io/...` one-liner.

## Acceptance
- Tagging a release publishes a multi-arch image.
- `docker pull` from another machine works on both architectures.
- Effort: M.

## References
- `.full-review/04b-cicd-findings.md` (CICD-2, CICD-14)

title: [Tracker] Security Medium/Low backlog (~14 findings)
description:
## Purpose
Umbrella tracker for remaining Medium/Low Security findings not in Sprint 1–3.

## Source
[`./full-review/02a-security-findings.md`](.full-review/02a-security-findings.md).

## Already filed elsewhere
- C-1, C-2, C-3, H-3, H-8 — closed at Phase-CP1.
- H-1 → `high-7`, H-2 → `high-1`, H-4 → `high-3`, H-5 → `high-2`, H-6 → `high-8`, H-7 → `high-9`.
- M-2, M-3 → `med-3`.
- M-5 → `high-11`.

## What's in here
~7 remaining Medium + ~6 Low Sec findings. Notable: HSTS header, Subresource Integrity on CDN assets (if any), cookie attribute tightening, log-secret scrubbing, error-message sanitization.

title: Split app.py into routers/{auth,scripts,folders,static}.py
description:
## Why
`backend/app.py` has grown into a god-module mixing auth, scripts, folders, static-file serving, middleware, lifespan, and `main`. Hard to navigate, hard to test in isolation, every PR touches the same file.

## What
Split into:
- `backend/routers/auth.py` — login/logout/status
- `backend/routers/scripts.py` — list/get/save/import/delete + active
- `backend/routers/folders.py` — list/create
- `backend/routers/static.py` — SPA fallback (or replace with StaticFiles per `med-2`)

Keep `app.py` ~50 LOC of `FastAPI()` composition, middleware registration, lifespan, and `if __name__`.

Do this **before** `med-2` and `med-3` so they edit small files, not the god-module.

## Acceptance
- All existing routes still serve at the same paths.
- pytest suite green.
- `wc -l backend/app.py` < 100.
- Effort: M.

## References
- `.full-review/01a-code-quality-findings.md` (CQ M-11)
- `.full-review/01b-architecture-findings.md` (Arch M2)
- `.full-review/04a-framework-findings.md` (Fwk M-2)

title: Add GZipMiddleware + TrustedHostMiddleware + security headers
description:
## Why
Three standard middlewares are missing:
1. **GZipMiddleware** — JSON responses are uncompressed; ~5x bandwidth saving on script listings.
2. **TrustedHostMiddleware** — blocks Host-header attacks (cache poisoning, password-reset poisoning if email features ever add).
3. **Security headers** — `X-Frame-Options: DENY`, `Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'`, `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff`, `Referrer-Policy: same-origin`, `Permissions-Policy: ...`.

## What
1. `app.add_middleware(GZipMiddleware, minimum_size=1024)`.
2. `app.add_middleware(TrustedHostMiddleware, allowed_hosts=os.getenv('AYS_ALLOWED_HOSTS', '*').split(','))`.
3. Custom small middleware (or `secure` package) emitting the headers above.
4. Document new env vars in README.

## Acceptance
- Response headers include the four above.
- Browser embed in an iframe is blocked.
- Effort: S.

## References
- `.full-review/02a-security-findings.md` (Sec M-2, Sec M-3)
- `.full-review/04a-framework-findings.md` (Fwk M-5)

title: [Tracker] Code Quality Medium/Low backlog (~29 findings)
description:
## Purpose
Umbrella tracker for the **remaining Medium and Low Code Quality findings** not in Sprint 1–3. Triage in batches when bandwidth permits; do **not** treat as a single PR.

## Source
[`./full-review/01a-code-quality-findings.md`](.full-review/01a-code-quality-findings.md) — Phase 1A reviewer output.

## Already filed elsewhere
- CQ C-1 (doc honesty) — closed at Phase-CP1.
- CQ M-11 (app.py god-module split) — covered by `med-1`.

## What's in here
~12 Medium + ~10 Low CQ findings. Themes: unused imports, missing docstrings, inconsistent naming, complex functions, dead code, magic numbers. Each line in the source file has a `file:line` anchor.

## Suggested workflow
When picking up: open the source file, pick 5–10 related items, file a thin PR. Close this tracker when the source file is exhausted (or replace with a smaller follow-up tracker).

title: [Tracker] All Low-severity findings (umbrella, ~45 items, 8 categories)
description:
## Purpose
Single umbrella for **all 45 Low-severity findings** across the 8 review categories. These are style nits / micro-optimizations / nice-to-haves that don't materially change the project's risk profile if deferred indefinitely.

## Source files (per-category breakdown)
- `.full-review/01a-code-quality-findings.md` (Low section)
- `.full-review/01b-architecture-findings.md` (Low section)
- `.full-review/02a-security-findings.md` (Low section)
- `.full-review/02b-performance-findings.md` (Low section)
- `.full-review/03a-testing-findings.md` (Low section)
- `.full-review/03b-documentation-findings.md` (Low section)
- `.full-review/04a-framework-findings.md` (Low section)
- `.full-review/04b-cicd-findings.md` (Low section)

## When to revisit
- Before tagging 1.0.
- When the Medium-category trackers above are cleared.
- If a specific Low finding becomes acutely visible (e.g., a Low security item gets cited in an external audit).

Do NOT file these individually unless one is being actively worked. Premature filing creates noise without delivering action.
